creator: mmel@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to John Baldwin from comment #6)
Oh, thanks. I think that I now know what's going on.
The dump is from ARM board, where nobody touched PCIe controller hardware until driver is loaded. Thus, the all IO or memory window descriptors are in their initial state so read returns mask and size.

The pcib_probe_windows() doesn't check this (unprogramed I/O window size) and "bad" things happens. And I'm near sure that you can replicate this behavior by setting  of hw.pci.clear_pcib tunnable to 1.

And my mistake was that I did not realize that pcib_probe_windows () is not intended for auto-allocation of I/O resources, sorry for this.

In any case, simple check in pcib_probe_windows() for this case can be sufficient, whats you mean? Autoconfig for I/O space looks like a purely superfluous.
